2603.20034.title: CoverageBench: Evaluating Information Coverage across Tasks and Domains
2603.20034.paper_summary: We wish to measure the information coverage of an ad hoc retrieval algorithm, that is, how much of the range of available relevant information is covered by the search results. Information coverage is a central aspect for retrieval, especially when the retrieval system is integrated with generative models in a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system. The classic metrics for ad hoc retrieval, precision and recall, reward a system as more and more relevant documents are retrieved. However, since relevance in ad hoc test collections is defined for a document without any relation to other documents that might contain the same information, high recall is sufficient but not necessary to ensure coverage. The same is true for other metrics such as rank-biased precision (RBP), normalized discounted cumulative gain (nDCG), and mean average precision (MAP). Test collections developed around the notion of diversity ranking in web search incorporate multiple aspects that support a concept of coverage in the web domain. In this work, we construct a suite of collections for evaluating information coverage from existing collections. This suite offers researchers a unified testbed spanning multiple genres and tasks. All topics, nuggets, relevance labels, and baseline rankings are released on Hugging Face Datasets, along with instructions for accessing the publicly available document collections.
2603.20034.paper_summary_zh: 我們希望測量一種特定檢索演算法的信息覆蓋率，即搜索結果覆蓋了多少可用的相關信息範圍。信息覆蓋率是檢索的一個核心方面，特別是當檢索系統與生成模型集成在檢索增強生成（RAG）系統中時。傳統的特定檢索指標——精確率和召回率——隨著檢索到的相關文檔越來越多而獎勵系統。然而，由於在特定測試集合中，文檔的相關性是針對單一文檔定義的，與可能包含相同信息的其他文檔無關，因此高召回率足以但不必要地確保覆蓋率。對於其他指標，如排名偏倚精確率（RBP）、標準化折扣累積增益（nDCG）和平均精確率（MAP），情況也是如此。圍繞網頁搜索中的多樣性排名概念開發的測試集合納入了支持網絡領域覆蓋概念的多個方面。在這項工作中，我們構建了一套用於評估信息覆蓋率的集合，基於現有的集合。這套集合為研究人員提供了一個統一的測試平台，涵蓋多種類型和任務。所有主題、亮點、相關性標籤和基準排名均在 Hugging Face Datasets 上發布，並附有訪問公開可用文檔集合的說明。
2603.20034.author: Saron Samuel et.al.
2603.20034.authors: Saron Samuel, Andrew Yates, Dawn Lawrie, Ian Soboroff, Trevor Adriaanse, Benjamin Van Durme, Eugene Yang
